rxn_class_summary: Electrochemical radical cyclization of 1,6-enynes with sulfonimides to construct benzofuran derivatives bearing aminomethyl groups
electrochemical_role: Anodic oxidation of sulfonimide (or its anion) to generate a nitrogen-centered sulfonamidyl radical that adds to the enyne

key_intermediates.name: vinyl/alkenyl radical from N-radical addition to alkene of 1,6-enyne
cyclized aryl/vinyl radical after intramolecular addition onto alkyne

electron_flow: Anode oxidizes the sulfonimide anion (generated with nBu4NOAc base) to a N-centered radical, which adds to the terminal alkene of the 1,6-enyne; the resulting C-radical cyclizes onto the tethered alkyne, and the vinyl radical is further oxidized to a cation that is trapped by methanol to give the dimethyl ketal/benzofuran product.

probe_reagents.name: TEMPO
probe_reagents.observation: Product 3a not detected (0% yield) under standard conditions with TEMPO added, consistent with a radical intermediate

probe_reagents.name: no-current control
probe_reagents.observation: Without electric current, product 3a was not formed (0% yield), confirming the electrochemical nature of the transformation

notes: Page also shows downstream synthetic applications: Ti(OiPr)4/Mg/Me3SiCl mediated N-S bond cleavage of 3a to monosulfonamide 5a (78%), and H2SO4-promoted hydrolysis/rearrangement of ketal 4a to ketone 6a (70%). These are derivatization steps, not part of the catalytic cycle.
